description: Fig. 15 presents Auger electron spectroscopy (AES) depth profiles of oxide films on Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steels (LDSs). Panel (a) displays the atomic concentration of O, Fe, Al, and Mn versus sputtering time (0–12 min) for as-hot-rolled S0 and solution-treated samples S950, S1050, and S1150. Panel (b) reorganizes the same data by element, enabling direct comparison across all four samples. All profiles exhibit oxygen enrichment at the surface that decays toward the substrate composition with increasing sputter depth. The oxide film thicknesses, determined at half-maximum oxygen concentration, are visibly annotated as 16.2 ± 0.8 nm for S0, 25.2 ± 1.2 nm for S950, 21.6 ± 1 nm for S1050, and 18 ± 0.9 nm for S1150. The S950 film is the thickest and uniformly Al-rich with low Fe and Mn throughout the film, whereas S0 shows comparable Fe and Al concentrations, and S1050/S1150 exhibit increasing Fe and decreasing Al with depth. The figure supports the core conclusion that fine-grained S950 develops a uniform, dense, Al-rich oxide film, and caption states that both oxide film thickness and Al content decrease with increasing grain size.
